title: Grammitis kookenamae
taxonomy: Grammitis kookenamae is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, which includes various species of ferns. This plant belongs to the genus Grammitis, a group of small to medium-sized epiphytic ferns. The classification of Grammitis kookenamae is further narrowed down to the order Polypodiales, a group of ferns characterized by their unique reproductive structures. At the kingdom level, Grammitis kookenamae is classified as Plantae, indicating its status as a vascular plant. The classification of Grammitis kookenamae is a testament to the complexity and diversity of plant life on Earth.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
